skill.name: obsidian-bases
skill.description: Create and edit Obsidian Bases files with valid YAML views, filters, formulas, summaries, and troubleshooting guidance.
skill.summary: Build structured Obsidian Bases views for notes using YAML, filters, formulas, and summaries.
skill.author: kepano

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution, weak-crypto, sensitive-file, and obfuscation patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown inline code, YAML examples, and Obsidian formula reference text. The only confirmed concern is normal user-directed filesystem modification because the skill creates .base files in an Obsidian vault. No prompt injection, credential access, executable code, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: User-Directed Vault File Creation
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to create .base files in an Obsidian vault and validate YAML. This is expected behavior for the skill, but it can modify user workspace files if used without review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly directs creation of .base files and validation in Obsidian. The action is user-facing and legitimate, but it still involves filesystem writes.

low_findings.title: Static Command-Execution Matches Are Markdown Syntax
low_findings.description: The analyzer reported many Ruby backtick and weak-crypto findings, but the reviewed locations are Markdown inline code, YAML examples, Obsidian formula functions, and reference tables. No executable Ruby, shell commands, credential access, or cryptographic implementation was found in the two scanned Markdown files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation using inline-code delimiters and YAML examples. The repository contains only SKILL.md and references/FUNCTIONS_REFERENCE.md, with no executable source files.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to official Obsidian help pages in the References section. They are documentation links, not code that performs network requests or sends user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plain Markdown links under a References heading. No fetch, curl, webhook, or data transfer instruction was found.

content.value_statement: Obsidian Bases can be difficult to write because filters, formulas, and YAML quoting must all be correct. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create valid .base files with clear views and summaries.
actual_capabilities: Creates .base files with valid YAML structure for Obsidian Bases.
Defines global and view-specific filters using tags, folders, properties, and dates.
Configures table, cards, list, and map views with ordered displayed properties.
Writes formulas for computed fields, date math, conditional labels, and summaries.
Explains YAML quoting rules and common formula errors for troubleshooting.
Uses a bundled function reference for date, string, number, list, file, link, object, and regex functions.
limitations: It does not run Obsidian or confirm that a view renders inside the app.
It depends on the notes and properties already present in the user vault.
Map views require latitude and longitude properties plus the Maps community plugin.
It can create or edit files, so users should review changes before applying them.

use_cases.title: Create a Task Dashboard
use_cases.description: Build a Base that filters task notes, shows due dates, groups by status, and summarizes priorities.

use_cases.title: Organize Research Notes
use_cases.description: Create table or card views for papers, books, sources, authors, reading status, and computed reading metrics.

use_cases.title: Index Daily Notes
use_cases.description: Generate a Base that filters daily notes by filename pattern and displays dates, word estimates, and modified times.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Base
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a first .base file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Obsidian Base for notes tagged #project. Show file name, status, owner, and due date in a table view.

prompt_templates.title: Add Filters and Views
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a .base file for my reading notes. Include one table for unread books and one cards view for favorites.

prompt_templates.title: Use Formulas and Summaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a task Base with formulas for overdue status, days until due, and priority labels. Add summaries for count and average priority.

prompt_templates.title: Debug an Existing Base
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this .base content for YAML quoting problems, missing formulas, invalid filters, and duration math mistakes.

output_examples.input: Create a Base for project notes with status, due date, and priority.
output_examples.output: A project dashboard .base file with a tag filter, table view, ordered project fields, and a due-date formula.

output_examples.input: Fix my Base that errors when calculating days until due.
output_examples.output: A corrected formula that checks for missing dates and reads the duration days field before rounding.

output_examples.input: Make a reading tracker with books grouped by reading status.
output_examples.output: A reading tracker Base with grouped table or cards views, author and page fields, and a reading-time formula.

best_practices: List the note properties you already use before asking for a Base.
Quote formulas carefully, especially when they contain double quotes or special YAML characters.
Test the generated .base file in Obsidian and report the exact error message when troubleshooting.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for formulas that reference properties that do not exist in your vault.
Do not use duration math without selecting a numeric field such as days or hours.
Do not rely on map views unless your notes include usable location properties.

faq.question: What is an Obsidian Base?
faq.answer: It is a .base file that creates database-like views of notes using filters, properties, formulas, and views.

faq.question: Can this skill edit existing .base files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review and update existing Base YAML when you provide the current content or file context.

faq.question: Does it support formulas?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for date math, conditionals, strings, numbers, lists, file functions, and summaries.

faq.question: Can it verify the Base inside Obsidian?
faq.answer: No. It can validate likely YAML and formula issues, but Obsidian must render the final view.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low risk only, mainly because the skill can create user-requested files in a vault.
